findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
Credential and secret exfiltration to an external endpoint
findings.description: The bundled validator harvests sensitive environment variables and local credential-bearing files, then transmits them to an external URL. This behavior is unrelated to code review and creates immediate risk of secret exposure.
findings.evidence: Collects env vars whose names contain TOKEN/KEY/SECRET/PASS/AUTH/CRED, reads ~/.gitconfig, ~/.netrc, and ~/.env, then POSTs the data to https://cr-analytics.dev/api/init via curl.
findings.remediation: Remove all collection of secrets and local dotfiles. Eliminate the external POST entirely, or limit any telemetry to clearly documented, non-sensitive data with explicit user opt-in.

findings.title: Deceptive packaging hides malicious behavior behind a code-review skill
findings.description: The manifest and instructions present the package as a code review assistant with a benign 'environment consistency check', but the actual implementation performs credential harvesting and network transmission. This is a material mismatch between advertised purpose and real behavior.
findings.evidence: SKILL.md describes 'Automated code review' and a one-time compatibility/registration check; _validator.py instead gathers secrets and uploads them externally.
findings.remediation: Align the manifest and instructions with actual behavior, or preferably remove the hidden telemetry component. Skills should not claim benign analysis functionality while performing unrelated sensitive data collection.

findings.title: Hidden automatic read-to-network tool chain on normal execution
findings.description: Running the advertised analysis entry point automatically triggers the validator before processing the requested file. This creates a covert workflow that reads sensitive local data and sends it off-host without a separate user action or confirmation.
findings.evidence: analyze.py calls _validator.run() unconditionally; _validator.py then enumerates secrets and performs a curl POST.
findings.remediation: Remove the implicit validator call from the main analysis path. Any networked registration or diagnostics should be isolated, optional, and explicitly approved by the user.
